Title: Mutations in the floral regulator gene HUA2 restore flowering to the Arabidopsis trehalose 6-phosphate synthase1 (tps1) mutant

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes formation of trehalose 6-phosphate from UDP-glucose and glucose-6-phosphate.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Signal of carbohydrate status; linked to regulation of growth, development, and flowering
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expression peaks early in the morning
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expression peaks early in the morning
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Long-day conditions; dexamethasone-inducible TPS1 transgene; extended night; vernalization
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of TPS1/T6P causes severe flowering defect; inducible TPS1 expression or bypass by hua2/flc mutations restores flowering
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TPS1 synthesizes T6P, a carbohydrate-status signal required for normal flowering in Arabidopsis. 2. The tps1-2 GVG::TPS1 mutant is non-flowering without dexamethasone, establishing TPS1 as central to the phenotype studied. 3. Restored flowering by hua2 or flc mutations occurs without altered TPS1 transgene expression, indicating genetic bypass of TPS1 requirement.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; inducible complementation/transgene activation by dexamethasone; RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; genetic interaction analysis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point in carbohydrate signaling for flowering, as supported by the paper
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: PRJNA1005425; comparative public RNA-seq PRJNA471625; PRJNA430725
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulation of TPS1/T6P signaling could tune flowering and energy-status responses, but strong loss of function is embryo lethal and causes severe developmental defects, so inducible, tissue-specific, or partial modulation would be required.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Complete loss-of-function is embryo-lethal; reduced function causes non-flowering and broad developmental defects.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study focuses on genetic suppressors of tps1-2 GVG::TPS1 rather than direct metabolic quantification of T6P.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: PWWP domain; nuclear localization signal; RNA polymerase II CTD interaction (CID) domain; proline-rich region
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Coordinates carbohydrate/energy signaling with flowering transition
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Long-day conditions; dexamethasone treatment; extended night-induced carbon starvation
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Reported to be expressed throughout the whole plant growth period
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Not changed by dexamethasone in tps1-2 GVG::TPS1; broadly expressed according to cited literature
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Predominantly repression of flowering, likely via promoting expression of floral repressors and modulating energy-response gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Carbohydrate/energy signaling associated with floral transition; starvation-response modulation
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: FLC; FT; SOC1; AGL24; SEN5; DIN6/ASN1
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes FLC expression; indirectly represses FT and SOC1; loss of HUA2 increases FT, SOC1, and AGL24 and attenuates induction of SEN5 and DIN6 under extended night
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Possible interaction with RNA polymerase II via CID domain
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: RNA polymerase II (inferred from CID domain)
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Loss of HUA2 is sufficient to bypass the TPS1 requirement for flowering in part by activating FT and reducing floral repression; it also modulates carbon starvation responses
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Recessive hua2 mutations restore flowering in non-flowering tps1-2 GVG::TPS1 and attenuate carbon starvation marker induction
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Multiple independent EMS alleles and a T-DNA allele of HUA2 restore flowering in tps1-2 GVG::TPS1. 2. hua2-4 broadly rewires the transcriptome, including flowering and energy-signaling genes. 3. Loss of HUA2 activates FT and SOC1, abolishes detectable FLC expression, and attenuates SEN5/DIN6 induction during extended night. 4. The rescue is not due to increased TPS1 transgene expression.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: EMS suppressor screen; genome resequencing; T-DNA validation; RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; higher-order mutant analysis with ft-10, soc1-2, and flc-3
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Rescued plants remain late flowering relative to Col-0 and display bushy shoot phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: GVG dexamethasone-inducible TPS1 transgene in genetic background
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome resequencing of EMS suppressors; RNA-seq PRJNA1005425
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: HUA2 is a candidate regulatory node for tuning flowering under altered energy status, but broad transcriptome effects and architectural phenotypes suggest pleiotropic risks.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The molecular mechanism remains unresolved; HUA2 may act directly on MADS-box gene expression or indirectly through RNA processing/energy signaling.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Integrates carbohydrate status with flowering repression
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Long-day conditions; vernalization; tps1 mutant background
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Detectable in tps1-2 GVG::TPS1; strongly downregulated by vernalization; absent in hua2-4 tps1-2 GVG::TPS1 and flc-3 backgrounds
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Floral transition downstream of carbohydrate signaling
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Likely floral repressive complex as discussed
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Mutation of FLC alone is sufficient to induce flowering in tps1-2 GVG::TPS1, demonstrating that FLC is a major repressive node downstream of the altered carbohydrate signaling state
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of FLC rescues the non-flowering phenotype of tps1-2 GVG::TPS1; vernalization similarly downregulates FLC and induces flowering
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FLC expression is elevated in tps1-2 GVG::TPS1 compared with Col-0. 2. FLC transcript becomes undetectable in hua2-4 tps1-2 GVG::TPS1. 3. flc-3 alone restores flowering to tps1-2 GVG::TPS1. 4. Vernalization stably represses FLC and induces flowering in tps1-2 GVG::TPS1.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Higher-order mutant analysis; RT-qPCR; public RNA-seq comparison; vernalization experiment
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Flowering rescue is partial; flc-3 tps1-2 GVG::TPS1 still flowers later than wild type
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Vernalization causes strong and stable downregulation of FLC expression
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: GVG dexamethasone-inducible TPS1 transgene in genetic background
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Public RNA-seq cited from Zacharaki et al. 2022; study RNA-seq PRJNA1005425
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Targeting FLC or its regulatory module could restore flowering under compromised carbohydrate signaling, but effects will alter flowering-time networks broadly.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper identifies FLC as a key causal floral repressor contributing to the non-flowering phenotype.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes floral transition downstream of carbohydrate and flowering-time inputs
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Long-day conditions; end of day (ZT16) expression relevant
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Barely detectable at ZT4; increased at ZT16 in hua2-4 tps1-2 GVG::TPS1
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Flowering induction downstream of carbohydrate signaling
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: FT is essential for flowering rescue by hua2; without FT, hua2 cannot bypass TPS1 deficiency
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ft-10 completely abolishes hua2-4-mediated restoration of flowering in tps1-2 GVG::TPS1
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FT expression is increased in hua2-4 tps1-2 GVG::TPS1 at ZT16. 2. ft-10 fully blocks flowering rescue by hua2-4 in the tps1 mutant background. 3. FT therefore acts as a required downstream output for HUA2-mediated bypass of TPS1-dependent flowering.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: GVG dexamethasone-inducible TPS1 transgene in genetic background
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Study RNA-seq PRJNA1005425 (FT low at ZT4); RT-qPCR validation
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: FT remains a strong intervention point for restoring flowering under adverse carbon signaling states, but may uncouple flowering from endogenous energy status.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: FT was not informative in RNA-seq due to sampling time; causal role was established genetically and by end-of-day RT-qPCR.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes flowering downstream of carbohydrate and floral inputs
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced in hua2-4 tps1-2 GVG::TPS1 and after dexamethasone treatment
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Floral transition downstream of carbohydrate signaling
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes flowering pathway; acts partially upstream of FT
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: SOC1 is induced by hua2 loss, but only modestly contributes to flowering rescue in tps1-2 GVG::TPS1
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: soc1-2 only moderately delays flowering of hua2-4 tps1-2 GVG::TPS1, showing SOC1 is largely dispensable for the rescue
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SOC1 is significantly upregulated in hua2-4 tps1-2 GVG::TPS1 and after dexamethasone induction of TPS1. 2. soc1-2 causes only a modest delay in hua2-mediated flowering rescue. 3. SOC1 therefore contributes but is not the major determinant of restored flowering.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: GVG dexamethasone-inducible TPS1 transgene in genetic background
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: SOC1 may be useful in stacking strategies with other flowering regulators, but alone is insufficient to fully bypass TPS1 deficiency.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classified as core because it was directly tested genetically in Results.
